title: RecommendSettingsRequest
description: Canonical request shape for runtime-aware solver setting recommendations.

AttributeBalanceMode.description: Mode for evaluating attribute balance targets.

oneOf.description: Penalize absolute deviation from the desired count (current behavior)
Penalize only when actual < desired; overshooting is allowed without penalty
Limits how often people can encounter each other across sessions

max_allowed_encounters.description: Maximum number of times two people can be in the same group

penalty_function.description: Penalty function type: "squared" for quadratic penalties, "linear" for linear penalties

penalty_weight.description: Weight of the penalty applied for constraint violations

oneOf.description: Maintains desired attribute distributions within specific groups

desired_values.description: Desired count for each attribute value (e.g., {"male": 2, "female": 2})

group_id.description: ID of the group where this balance constraint applies

mode.description: How to interpret desired counts. `Exact` penalizes deviation in either direction, `AtLeast` penalizes only shortfalls (overshoot is not penalized).

penalty_weight.description: Weight of the penalty applied for balance violations

sessions.description: Optional list of session indices in which this constraint is active. If `None`, the constraint applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Fixes specific people to specific groups in specific sessions

group_id.description: ID of the group where this person must be placed

person_id.description: ID of the person who must be fixed in place

sessions.description: List of session indices where this person must be in the specified group. If `None`, applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Keeps specified people in the same group

people.description: List of person IDs that must stay together

sessions.description: Optional list of session indices where this constraint applies. If `None`, applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Prefers specified people to be in the same group (soft constraint)

penalty_weight.description: Penalty weight when the people are not together

people.description: List of person IDs that should be together

sessions.description: Optional list of session indices where this constraint applies. If `None`, applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Prevents specified people from being in the same group

penalty_weight.description: Penalty weight for violations (higher = more important)

people.description: List of person IDs that should not be together

sessions.description: Optional list of session indices where this constraint applies. If `None`, applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Fixes a *set* of people to a specific group in specific sessions (hard constraint)

group_id.description: ID of the group where these people must be placed

people.description: IDs of the people who must be fixed in place

sessions.description: List of session indices where these people must be in the specified group. If `None`, applies to all sessions.

oneOf.description: Constrains a pair's meeting count across a fixed subset of sessions

mode.description: Penalty mode: at_least (default), exact, or at_most

people.description: Exactly two person IDs involved in the constraint

sessions.description: Sessions to consider for counting meetings (must be within problem.sessions)

target_meetings.description: Target number of meetings within the provided sessions (0..=sessions.len())

Group.description:
Represents a group that people can be assigned to.

Each group has a unique identifier and a fixed capacity that limits how many people can be assigned to it in any single session.

# Example

```no_run use solver_core::models::Group;

let team = Group { id: "Development Team".to_string(), size: 6, // Can hold up to 6 people }; ```

id.description: Unique identifier for this group (must be unique across all groups)

size.description: Maximum number of people that can be assigned to this group in any session

Objective.description:
Defines an optimization objective with its weight.

Objectives specify what the solver should optimize for. Multiple objectives can be specified with different weights to create a multi-objective optimization.

# Supported Objective Types

- `"maximize_unique_contacts"`: Maximize the number of unique person-to-person interactions

# Example

```no_run use solver_core::models::Objective;

let objective = Objective { r#type: "maximize_unique_contacts".to_string(), weight: 1.0, }; ```

type.description: The type of objective to optimize for

weight.description: Weight of this objective in the overall optimization function

PairMeetingMode.description: Modes for how to penalize deviations from the target meeting count.

oneOf.description: Penalize only shortfalls: weight * max(0, target - actual)
Penalize only excess: weight * max(0, actual - target)

attributes.description: Key-value attributes used for constraint evaluation (e.g., "gender" -> "female")

id.description: Unique identifier for this person (must be unique across all people)

sessions.description: Optional list of session indices this person participates in. If `None`, the person participates in all sessions. Session indices are 0-based (first session is 0).

ProblemDefinition.description:
Defines the core optimization problem: people, groups, and sessions.

This structure specifies the fundamental elements that need to be scheduled: the list of people to be assigned, the groups they can be assigned to, and how many scheduling sessions will occur.

groups.description: List of all available groups with their capacity limits

num_sessions.description: Total number of scheduling sessions (time periods)

people.description: List of all people to be scheduled into groups
